findings.description: The central tag diagram with surrounding callout annotations was not preserved as a diagrammatic layout.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the page centers a boxed shellstock tag mockup with explanatory notes arranged around it and connected by arrows. In the HTML, this is converted into a single linear text flow under 'SALE TAG', losing the original spatial grouping and annotated structure.

findings.description: Graphic content from the annotated tag illustration is missing as an image/diagram.
findings.evidence: The PDF contains a prominent visual graphic: a tag illustration with arrows pointing to fields and explanatory labels on both sides. The HTML contains only text equivalents and no visible image or reconstructed graphic in the corresponding section.

findings.description: Some formatting hierarchy and label styling from the tag example is simplified in the HTML.
findings.evidence: The PDF emphasizes the mock tag with boxed borders, centered text blocks, and stronger visual separation between field labels and sample values. The HTML preserves bold/italic in places but flattens alignment and removes the boxed tag presentation, reducing distinction between sample tag content and explanatory notes.

findings.description: The HTML omits the instructional callout labels and annotations that appear around the sample tag in the PDF image.
findings.evidence: PDF includes side annotations such as “Dealer Name, Address, & Certification Number,” “Certification Number of original shipper of the shellfish IF that shipper is different from the shipper noted at top of tag,” “Type of shellfish: oysters, clams, or mussels. Quantity can include number, volume, or weight,” “90 Day tag retention notice,” “Notice that product must remain refrigerated,” “Consumer Advisory Notice,” and “Space for Reshipper...” placed around the tag. HTML preserves many of these as italic explanatory notes under fields, but not the full visual annotation structure/arrows as a distinct block.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy does not accurately reflect the PDF structure; the sample tag label is promoted to a section heading.
findings.evidence:
PDF shows one main title, “SHELLSTOCK TAG INFORMATION REQUIREMENTS,” and the words “SALE” and “TAG” as labels within the illustrated sample tag, not as document section headings. HTML uses

SHELLSTOCK TAG INFORMATION REQUIREMENTS

and

SALE TAG

, which changes document structure by turning tag labels into a heading.

findings.description: There are text accuracy errors in punctuation/wording that slightly alter the original wording of the tag and footer text.
findings.evidence: PDF footer reads: “ALL SHELLSTOCK TAGS MUST BE MADE OF WATERPROOF, TEAR RESISTANT MATERIAL, MUST CONTAIN LEGIBLE INFORMATION, AND MUST BE AT LEAST 5 ¼” X 2 5/8” or Tags be at least 13.8 square inches (89.03 cm2) in size.” HTML reproduces this but normalizes quotes and may slightly alter typographic symbols. More notably, PDF tag line reads “RETAILERS: DATE WHEN LAST SHELLFISH FROM THIS CONTAINER SOLD OR SERVED ________________.” HTML renders “____________________.” The sample tag in PDF displays “SALE” and “TAG” separately, while HTML merges them conceptually as “SALE TAG.” These are not fabrications but are text/structure deviations.
